headline: S&P 500 volatility flashing mixed signals
detail: Bulls and bears are evenly matched right now with no dominant trend. For a margin-heavy tech portfolio, a directionless choppy market is dangerous — it grinds down leveraged positions without giving clean entry or exit points. Stay defensive until a clearer trend emerges.

headline: SpaceX investors grapple with Elon-driven volatility
detail: The 'cult of Elon' headline directly flags that SPCX and SPAL are subject to sharp, sentiment-driven swings beyond normal IPO risk. SPAL is 2x leveraged on top of that, so a bad news cycle on Musk could hit it twice as hard. Watch these closely and keep position sizes small.

headline: Five reasons the U.S. dollar is rising
detail: A strengthening dollar is a quiet tax on global tech and chip revenue — AVGO and PLTR both earn significantly outside the U.S. This adds another layer of pressure on your two biggest losers and is a concrete reason to take the TRIM signal on AVGO seriously now.

headline: Earnings exuberance becoming a problem — Wall Street veteran
detail: A veteran analyst is warning that the market is pricing in too much earnings optimism, which sets up disappointment risk. Your chip and tech names like AVGO and CRWD are exactly the high-expectation stocks that get hit hardest when earnings merely meet rather than blow past estimates.
